sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, event_date DATE, event_name VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_date, event_name) VALUES (1, DATE '2023-01-01', 'New Year'), (2, DATE '2023-02-15', 'Valentine''s Day'), (3, DATE '2023-03-20', 'Spring Equinox');
SELECT monthname(event_date - INTERVAL (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates) DAY) AS adjusted_month, COUNT(*) FROM test_dates GROUP BY adjusted_month HAVING adjusted_month IS NOT NULL;

SELECT t1.id, (t1.event_date - INTERVAL 30 DAY) AS old_date, monthname((t1.event_date - INTERVAL 30 DAY)) AS old_month FROM test_dates t1 JOIN (SELECT id, event_date FROM test_dates WHERE event_name LIKE 'Valentine%') t2 ON t1.event_date = t2.event_date;
SELECT monthname(NULLIF(event_date, DATE '2023-02-15')) AS month, COUNT(*) AS event_count FROM test_dates GROUP BY month HAVING month IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE additional_dates (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, start_date TIMESTAMP, end_date TIMESTAMP);

INSERT INTO additional_dates (id, start_date, end_date) VALUES (1, TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 00:00:00', TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 01:00:00'), (2, TIMESTAMP '2023-02-15 12:30:00', TIMESTAMP '2023-02-15 13:30:00');
SELECT monthname(TIMESTAMP '2023-03-27 00:00:00' - INTERVAL 1 MONTH) AS previous_month, COUNT(*) FROM additional_dates GROUP BY previous_month;
SELECT event_name, monthname(event_date + INTERVAL 1 MONTH) AS next_month FROM test_dates WHERE event_date > DATE '2023-01-01' ORDER BY event_date DESC;

SELECT t1.event_name, (t1.event_date - INTERVAL 1 DAY) AS previous_day, monthname(previous_day) AS prev_month FROM test_dates t1 WHERE t1.event_date IN (SELECT event_date FROM test_dates WHERE event_name = 'New Year');
